summary: Pristine soundboard recording of a relaxed 1976 show highlighted by a soaring Peggy‑O, the final “Mission in the Rain,” and tight, jam‑filled second set.
review: The transfer from the Betty (sbd) board is described as flawless – clear, well‑balanced and far superior to any audience source.  Musically the concert is consistently praised for its laid‑back pacing, standout renditions of Peggy‑O, Mission in the Rain, Tennessee Jed and Row Jimmy, plus a superb second‑set flow featuring Lazy Lightning/Supplication, St Stephen and The Wheel.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 40 reviews overwhelmingly positive (mostly 5‑star) praising both the recording fidelity and the performance; only a single mixed review mentions tempo, so overall rating is driven by excellent show quality (≈70%) plus pristine soundboard audio (≈30%).

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as ‘pristine’, ‘fantastic’, and superior to the audience version; no major drop‑outs mentioned, only occasional comment about slight coloration that is still considered “just right.”

show_quality.setlist_flow: A relaxed, ‘no‑hurry’ vibe with smooth segues – strong opener, a second set built around extended jams and a memorable closing sequence; many reviewers note the perfect pacing and energy build.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his guitar tone, slide work and vocal phrasing (e.g., ‘Travis Bean sound’, ‘slightly drags out the words’).
band_member_comments.Phil: One reviewer highlights crystal‑clear bass on a cassette source, noting Phil’s presence throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s playing is called out as “magical” and “on fire,” especially on the ‘76 sound that sits between ’74 and ’77.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drummers are noted for tight, energetic playing – launching NFA, driving jams in The Wheel and overall solid rhythmic foundation.
